live_path.role: parse CLI args and forward --owner into the service layer
orchestrate owner resolution, record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission
derive effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or config defaults
materialize the base record with name, status, and resolved owner only
derive routing_key from the resolved owner and item name
emit the final payload by copying the base record and adding owner_source and routing_key

owner_source.rule: return "explicit" when a non-empty owner argument is provided; otherwise load config/defaults.json owner and return "default"
owner_source.storage_layer_involvement: sync_app.store.make_record does not derive or persist owner_source on the live path

routing_key.rule: slugify owner and name, then join as owner:name
routing_key.ordering: computed after sync_app.cli.main passes --owner into sync_app.service.sync_item and after effective_owner is resolved

emission.rule: copy the base record, append owner_source and routing_key, then return json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True)

test_observations.observation: explicit owner input produces owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist
missing owner input falls back to config/defaults.json owner and emits owner_source=default
the CLI path preserves the same derived fields seen in the service-level explicit-owner case

rejected_decoys.reason: helper is never called on the live path; rg only finds its definition
legacy helper is never called on the live path; rg only finds its definition
legacy helper is never called on the live path; rg only finds its definition
historical note conflicts with the current implementation and tests
